skill.name: pptx-prep
skill.description: Pre-checks presentation requests before PPTX generation by identifying missing names, photos, logos, data, and other human-supplied materials. It creates a structured manifest so downstream PPTX tools can generate slides with fewer hallucinated or missing assets.
skill.summary: Pre-check presentation materials and prepare a manifest before PPTX generation.
skill.author: xiaomengbi520

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high patterns, but review found most are false positives from manifest field names, examples, markdown code fences, badges, and schema URLs. The real risks are legitimate but elevated workflow behaviors: creating and scanning a materials directory, reading user-provided files, and optionally searching or generating images for missing assets.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Material Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to create a materials directory, scan user files, auto-match content, and write manifest.yml. This is expected for the skill, but it requires user awareness because files in the project may contain private presentation assets. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for filesystem access risk, not malicious intent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly creates, scans, and records local project files. The behavior is central to the skill and bounded to project materials, so confidence is high for risk presence but not for abuse.

medium_findings.title: Optional Web Search and Image Generation Downloads
medium_findings.description: For missing presentation materials, the skill can search for real images or generate images, download results into materials, and mark them in the manifest. This introduces network and provenance risk if users choose those options. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for network-enabled workflow risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly call for search or image generation and saving results. The actions require user choice and confirmation, which reduces severity.

medium_findings.title: Local Manifest Validator Reads User-Supplied Files
medium_findings.description: validate.py opens a path supplied on the command line and parses YAML or JSON manifests. This is normal validator behavior, but users should run it only on trusted local manifest files. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for file read capability, low evidence of exploitation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly reads the requested file and does not perform network calls or command execution. The risk is limited to local file handling and malformed input parsing.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Flags Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography hits occur on manifest and prompt terms such as status, source, confidence, path, and examples. I found no hashing, encryption, signing, or weak crypto API use in the reviewed files. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited contexts define presentation material metadata rather than cryptographic operations. Static line hits align with schema and YAML fields, not crypto code.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Execution Flags Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command findings are mostly markdown code fences, inline code, and usage examples. The skill does not contain Ruby code or shell backtick execution logic. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for Ruby execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are documentation or prompt templates. The visible content is fenced examples and literal paths, not executable Ruby backticks.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Findings Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are schema identifiers and README badge image URLs. They do not perform runtime network requests by themselves. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for active network behavior, with minor documentation tracking risk for badge images.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are static metadata or badge references. They are not fetched by skill code unless a renderer loads README images.

low_findings.title: Critical Dangerous Combination Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The scanner reported code execution plus network plus credential access, but review found no credential harvesting or exfiltration evidence in the cited files. Network and filesystem behavior exist, but they are presentation-material workflow features. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for confirmed malicious combination.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: I found concrete workflow use of files and optional network search, but no credential access semantics. Confidence is high enough to dismiss the critical claim while retaining medium risk.

content.user_title: Prepare Presentation Materials Before PPTX Generation
content.value_statement: Presentation generators often invent names, photos, data, and logos when source materials are missing. This skill checks requirements first, collects decisions, and produces a manifest for cleaner PPTX generation.
actual_capabilities: Identifies human-dependent presentation materials such as names, photos, logos, data, and references.
Asks users to confirm slide structure before material collection begins.
Creates a manifest.yml file that records material status, source, confidence, and slide usage.
Checks photo aspect ratio, low resolution, and content fit before handoff.
Supports placeholders, AI-drafted text, AI image search, and AI-generated image decisions.
Includes a Python validator for pptx-prep manifest files.
limitations: It does not generate PPTX files by itself.
It depends on a separate PPTX generation tool for final slide creation.
AI-searched or AI-generated materials still need user review for accuracy and rights.
Automatic file matching can be wrong when filenames or image content are ambiguous.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Team Presentation
use_cases.description: Check missing names, photos, contact details, and project facts before creating a group deck.

use_cases.title: Plan a Pitch Deck
use_cases.description: Separate verified company assets from AI-drafted market text and placeholder financial data.

use_cases.title: Standardize Presentation Handoff
use_cases.description: Create a structured material manifest so downstream slide tools know which assets belong on each slide.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Simple Deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pptx-prep to check what materials I need for a 5-slide presentation about [topic]. Ask me for missing human-provided items before generation.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Uploaded Materials
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pptx-prep for my presentation. I will place photos, logos, and data files in materials/. Match each file to the correct slide and show me the full table.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Missing Assets
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pptx-prep to review this deck outline. For each missing image or data point, give me the options to upload, use a placeholder, search, or generate.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Handoff Manifest
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced handoff to another PPTX tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full pptx-prep workflow for this pitch deck. Produce manifest.yml with source, confidence, slide usage, warnings, and placeholder decisions.

output_examples.input: I need a 7-slide team presentation, but I only have topic notes.
output: A confirmed slide structure with missing human-provided items marked.
A material scan showing team names, photos, dates, and contact details that need user input.
A warning if more than half of required materials are missing.

output_examples.input: I added logo.png, team.jpg, and revenue.xlsx to materials/.
output: A matching table that maps each file to presentation slots.
Aspect ratio and quality warnings for images that may crop or blur.
A final manifest summary showing ready items, placeholders, and items needing review.

output_examples.input: I do not have an award photo for slide 6.
output: A choice between AI search, AI generation, placeholder, or later upload.
A user confirmation step before any searched or generated image is used.
A manifest entry that records the selected source and review status.

best_practices: Confirm the slide outline before collecting assets.
Place user-provided materials in the project materials folder with clear filenames.
Review all AI-sourced text and images before sending the deck to an audience.
anti_patterns: Do not let AI invent names, logos, financial data, or personal photos.
Do not hand off a manifest that still contains unresolved missing or ai-fill items.
Do not ignore image size, aspect ratio, or content mismatch warnings.

faq.question: Does this skill generate PowerPoint files?
faq.answer: No. It prepares materials and a manifest for a separate PPTX generation tool.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What files does it create?
faq.answer: It can create a materials folder and a manifest.yml file in the project directory.

faq.question: Can it use AI to fill missing content?
faq.answer: Yes, but AI-sourced content is marked for review or saved as a placeholder decision.

faq.question: How does it reduce hallucinations?
faq.answer: It separates verified user materials from items that AI may draft or must not invent.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it reads project files and can use web or image tools.
